explanatory_strength.rationale: Operating mechanics remain mapped (hub scale, OCF versus GAAP net income, loyalty co-brand, leverage stack) with cyclical versus structural labeling explicit. Competitive-advantage mechanism is still absent: moat is none, fare competition and mandatory fleet capex cap pricing power, and the mispricing thesis is weak when proof-first component base is negative $9.79 per share while synthesis IRR is 28.48%. Round-two research did not supply any filing-grounded loyalty segment economics or ROIC on fleet capex; the dive still cannot distinguish why a levered commodity airline stub should compound like a quality franchise.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q anchor revenue, margins, OCF, capex, debt, and share count; calculation proofs are 100% complete with clean overlap keys. Round-two research_response confirms the frozen packet still lacks year-by-year debt maturities, interest expense, weighted-average coupon, covenant headroom, and management disclosure (management_facts document_count zero). AAdvantage standalone economics remain judgment-only ($0 to $20 per share band), owner-cash normalization splits between $4.70 per share OCF proxy and $3.10 per share Lawrence FCF without a filing bridge, and incremental ROIC on $3.8B fleet capex is not disclosed.

downside_control.rationale: Net debt near $28.1B against $902M cash and $3.7B equity bounds liquidity; cycle reserve and net financial claims are quantified at negative $9.00 and negative $42.67 per share base. Research loop confirms near-term liquidity is thin ($902M cash versus $3.753B current maturities) while FY2025 capex exceeded OCF, and the packet cannot compute interest coverage or stress-refinance paths. Downside is not controlled at the equity layer: recession plus credit tightening can force dilutive refinancing before deleveraging converts OCF to equity value.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Proof-first contract base economic value is negative $9.79 per share at $15.14 with null base annualized return; only the high component case reaches 8.12% per year, far below a ~15% hurdle. Lawrence synthesis 28.48% and bull 35% rely on scenario weights (48% bull) not grounded in filing-locked component sum; bear scenario 15.2% still assumes recovery on a no-moat levered stub. Hohn return screen fails on both absolute base economics and upside/downside asymmetry.

claims.claim: Proof-first additive component schedule sums to negative $9.79 per share base ($33.88 mid-cycle network + $8.00 co-brand option − $42.67 net financial claims − $9.00 cycle reserve) versus price $15.14, so filing-locked economics do not support a positive base return at spot.

Round-two research_response confirms the frozen packet cannot supply debt maturity schedules, interest expense, covenant headroom, or stress-refinance analysis; $902M cash against $3.753B current maturities while FY2025 capex of $3.779B exceeded OCF of $3.099B leaves equity recovery versus dilution unbounded under credit tightening.

AAdvantage co-brand option base $8.00 per share ($5.3B milestone) is pure judgment with no filing-disclosed standalone segment economics; capitalization at 7.2× on OCF that includes ticket revenue from redeemed miles risks partial double-count with the co-brand option despite overlap_key separation.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market correctly prices American as a commoditized, highly levered network airline with no durable moat: operating cash must service ~$29 billion of debt and mandatory fleet renewal before equity captures normalized owner cash, so the stub is a volatile option on mid-cycle recovery and deleveraging rather than a mispriced quality compounder.
most_important_missing_fact: Standalone AAdvantage co-brand cash economics (annual partner mile sales, margin, and renewal terms) from SEC filings or management disclosure, to test whether loyalty is a separable competitive advantage or already fully embedded in consolidated operating cash flow.
falsifiers: Trailing four-quarter operating cash flow per share falls below $3.50 for four consecutive quarters without proportional net debt reduction below $26 billion
Operating margin compresses below 2% while total debt and lease obligations exceed $32 billion for two consecutive quarters
Disclosed co-brand renewal economics imply incremental loyalty value below $4 per share, collapsing the separate AAdvantage option component
specialist_findings: Competitive-advantage audit finds no durable moat (classification moat=none; capital_cycle archetype). Round-two research loop status unresolved on credit bridge; cycle_leverage_reserve (−$9.00/sh base) is judgment not tied to interest burden or maturity concentration. Overlap controls remain clean: midcycle_passenger_network, aadvantage_co_brand_option, net_financial_claims, and cycle_leverage_reserve use unique overlap keys with empty double_counting_flags. Audit concerns unchanged: (1) owner-cash anchor splits between $4.70/sh OCF proxy and $3.10/sh Lawrence FCF without reconciliation; (2) co-brand base is judgment-only; (3) 7.2× capitalization on OCF risks partial double-count with loyalty option; (4) synthesis IRR 28.48% weights bull 48% while proof-first base economic value is negative; (5) no incremental ROIC ledger on fleet capex; (6) no management fieldwork. Hohn power-zone fit remains poor: capital_cycle levered stub with moat=none fails competitive-advantage and positive FCF yield screens.
